behaviors.description: Conversation history sent to the LLM is truncated to the latest 10 messages when history is too long.
behaviors.justification: Slot A: "history passed to the LLM is trimmed to the most recent 10 messages". Slot B: handoff boundary (history builder -> LLM client payload). Slot C: if lowered to Unit, a real wiring bug where trimming exists but untrimmed history is actually sent would be missed. Integration-brake checks: no real OS/network/subprocess required, no model-output-quality dependence, no multi-agentic chain.
behaviors.test_description: Run the request pipeline with >10 prior messages, replace the LLM transport with an in-process capture stub, and assert the outbound payload contains exactly the latest 10 messages in order.

behaviors.description: Generated response is relevant to the customer’s question (not generic/off-topic).
behaviors.justification: Slot A: "addresses the customer's question rather than producing a generic or off-topic reply". Slot B: model-output boundary (LLM judgment/content quality). Slot C: if lowered to Integration, deterministic stubs can always return relevant text and hide real relevance failures from the live model. Rejection gate for Workflow: behavior asks for single-response relevance, not multiple agentic decisions in sequence.
behaviors.test_description: Execute real LLM calls on a representative prompt set with rubric-based relevance scoring (or human-validated labels) and fail when responses are generic/off-topic beyond threshold.

behaviors.description: Tone re-check detects overly formal output for casual-tier customers and causes regeneration.
behaviors.justification: Slot A: "correctly identifies when a response is too formal... and triggers regeneration". Slot B: model-output boundary (tone classification decision) plus orchestration trigger. Slot C: if lowered to Integration, a mocked tone checker would always classify correctly and miss real misclassification that prevents regeneration. Rejection gate for Workflow: text does not require validating quality across multiple model decisions, only that one classification decision triggers regeneration.
behaviors.test_description: Use real model-based tone-checking with seeded overly formal drafts for casual-tier cases, then assert regeneration path is invoked when the live checker flags formality.

behaviors.description: User message, initial response, and any regenerated responses are stored with correct timestamps.
behaviors.justification: Slot A: "full exchange ... is persisted to the database with correct timestamps". Slot B: handoff boundary (chat orchestration -> repository/DB writes). Slot C: if lowered to Unit, per-function assertions can miss transactional/order/timestamp propagation errors across modules. Integration-brake checks: local DB wiring is sufficient; no real OS/network/subprocess requirement and no dependence on model output quality.
behaviors.test_description: Run the service against a real local test database, trigger both single-pass and regeneration flows, and assert persisted rows include all exchanges with expected timestamp fields/order.

behaviors.description: If regeneration is triggered, only the second response is exposed to the customer; rejected draft is never returned.
behaviors.justification: Slot A: "When the tone check triggers regeneration, the second response replaces the first". Slot B: handoff/state boundary (regen state -> response selection/return path). Slot C: if lowered to Unit, isolated selector logic can pass while real pipeline still leaks first draft due to module-ordering/state bugs. Rejection gate for Agentic: phrase "When ... triggers" makes model decision a precondition; this behavior targets replacement/visibility logic, not whether the model correctly decides to trigger.
behaviors.test_description: In an end-to-end in-process pipeline test, force the tone-check result to trigger regeneration, then assert API/output channel returns only the regenerated response and never the first draft.
